name: Members Trust Of The Southwest
description_short: Houston-based federal credit union established in 1936, serving 8,300+ members with checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services across 1 branch and 2 ATMs.
description_long:
Members Trust of the Southwest Federal Credit Union was established in 1936 and is headquartered in Houston, Texas. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates under the cooperative financial model, prioritizing member service over profit maximization. The institution has grown to serve over 8,300 members with a dedicated staff of 28 employees and manages $236.5 million in assets, positioning it as a stable mid-sized financial cooperative in the Houston market.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, business loans, and mortgage financing. Members can access digital banking through online platforms and mobile apps available on iOS and Android. Additional services include bill pay, wire transfers, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and financial calculators. The institution also offers IRA/KEOGH accounts and student scholarships, demonstrating community-focused financial planning tools.

Members Trust of the Southwest distinguishes itself through its long operational history spanning nearly 90 years and its strong asset base relative to membership size. The credit union maintains a focused presence with a single main office location at 2315 Mangum Road in Houston, supported by 2 ATMs for member convenience. Its digital infrastructure includes online banking and mobile app capabilities, enabling members to manage accounts remotely. The Q&A community forum provides member-to-member financial guidance and expert insights.

A key limitation is the minimal physical footprint—only 1 branch and 2 ATMs—which may inconvenience members seeking in-person services or those without nearby access. The website indicates the lobby is currently closed, and there are no member reviews posted, which limits transparency about member satisfaction. The institution's membership eligibility criteria appear restrictive, based on "common bonds," though specific details are not clearly outlined on the publicly available content. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established in 1936 with 90 years of operational history and stability
Manages $236.5 million in assets, providing financial security and scale
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, auto loans, business loans, and investment accounts
Mobile banking app available on both iOS and Android platforms
Digital services including remote deposit capture, e-statements, and online bill pay
Community-focused offerings such as student scholarships and member Q&A forum
Competitive account options with 8,600+ regular savings accounts and 1,400+ CDs among membership
cons: Only 1 branch location in Houston with 2 ATMs total, limiting physical accessibility
Lobby currently closed according to website, restricting in-person member service options
No member reviews available on profile, providing limited transparency on member satisfaction
Membership eligibility based on unspecified "common bonds," potentially restricting who can join
Website content appears incomplete—mortgage section cuts off mid-sentence, suggesting outdated information
best_for: Houston-based individuals and organizations meeting membership eligibility criteria seeking full-service banking
Members comfortable with digital-first banking due to limited physical branch access
Businesses and individuals seeking comprehensive loan products including auto, personal, and business financing
Savers looking for competitive CD rates and money market accounts through a credit union structure
diagnosis: Members Trust of the Southwest is best suited for Houston-area members who qualify under the credit union's membership eligibility criteria and are comfortable managing accounts primarily through digital channels given the single-branch, limited-ATM footprint. The main caveat is that membership appears restricted by unspecified "common bonds," and the closed lobby status raises questions about current service availability and operational status.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
